book_summary: James 1-5 has full chapter coverage with source-audited-draft restored translation and mystical companion layers. The book preserves embodied wisdom: endurance, wisdom, doing the word, care for vulnerable people, anti-favoritism, mercy over judgment, living trust through works, speech ethics, wisdom from above, humility, anti-oppressive wealth critique, prayer, anointing, healing, and restoration of wandering siblings.
translation_notes: James emphasizes embodied doing, not mere hearing or verbal belief.
Faith/trust and works should not be flattened into later polemical binaries; James presents living trust completed in action.
Pure reverence is care for orphans and widows in pressure.
Speech ethics depend on humans being made in Divine likeness.
James 5 targets exploitative wealth and withheld wages, not ordinary provision or survival needs.
Healing/prayer language should be pastoral and communal without mechanical guarantees.
source_witness_audit_flags: James 2 faith/works terminology: compare with Pauline usage in second pass.
James 5 healing/anointing: add pastoral-care and reception-history safeguards.
next_actions: Continue Batch B with 1 Peter and 2 Peter.
